title: Test a GFCI Outlet
description: Verify a ground-fault circuit interrupter outlet with a plug-in tester.

dialogue.text: GFCI outlets guard against shocks. Make sure your hands are dry, shoes are on, and the wall plate is intact before we start. Never remove outlet covers while the circuit is energized.
Confirm threshold criteria before testing: PASS means the normal wiring light pattern appears, pressing TEST trips power within 1 second, and RESET restores normal pattern without flicker.

Insert the tester fully and read the pattern. Press TEST and observe trip timing; then press RESET and confirm power restoration. Stop immediately if you smell burning, hear arcing, or the tester indicates open ground/hot-neutral reverse.

options.text: PASS: normal pattern, trip <=1s, reset stable.
FAIL: bad pattern, no trip, or reset instability.

dialogue.text: Fault branch: de-energize at the breaker, tag the outlet as unsafe, and inspect for loose conductors or load miswiring. Do not return the outlet to service until corrected and re-tested.

options.text: Corrective action completed; re-run full test cycle.

dialogue.text: Good result. Record that the outlet trips and resets correctly under the defined timing and pattern checks.
Nice work! The GFCI is wired correctly and trips as expected. Repeat monthly and replace outlets that fail any step.
